diagrams.title: Command Line Arguments Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how argc and argv are stored in memory when program is run as './program hello world 123'
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how arguments are stored and accessed in memory

diagrams.title: Valid vs Invalid Argument Access
diagrams.description: Illustrates the valid range of argv indices and consequences of accessing beyond argc-1
elements: argv[0] through argv[argc-1] are SAFE to access
Accessing argv[argc] or higher is UNDEFINED BEHAVIOR
Always validate: if (argc < expected+1) { error; exit; }
diagrams.purpose: Remembering to validate arguments before access

diagrams.title: String to Number Conversion Functions
diagrams.description: Shows conversion functions from command line strings to numeric types
diagrams.purpose: Choosing appropriate conversion function based on needs
